name: documentation-and-adrs
description_raw: Records decisions and documentation. Use when making architectural decisions, changing public APIs, shipping features, or when you need to record context that future engineers and agents will need to understand the codebase.
description_normalized: Records architectural decisions and context (why, not just what) via ADRs so future engineers and agents can understand the codebase.

frontmatter_yaml.name: documentation-and-adrs
frontmatter_yaml.description: Records decisions and documentation. Use when making architectural decisions, changing public APIs, shipping features, or when you need to record context that future engineers and agents will need to understand the codebase.

name: agent-orchestrator
description_raw: Design advisor for multi-agent orchestrated workflows using Claude Code teammate mode. Use when user wants to "design a multi-agent workflow", "set up agent orchestration", "spin up multiple agents", "build an agent pipeline", "orchestrate agents", "agent team workflow", "teammate mode setup", "how do I run multiple agents", "agent sequencing", "multi-agent gates".
description_normalized: Design advisor for multi-agent orchestrated workflows using Claude Code teammate mode.

name: agentic-capability
description_raw: Design advisor for choosing the right Claude Code extension mechanism before building anything. Covers the full agentic toolkit — skills, commands, subagents, hooks, and agent teams. Use when user asks "should I build a skill or a command?", "what's the best way to implement this?", "should this be a subagent?", "how should I structure these skills?", "I want to build X what should I use?", "skill vs command", "how do I add this capability", "design this agentic capability", "what mechanism should I use", "agentic capability", "what should I build".
description_normalized: Design advisor for choosing the right Claude Code extension mechanism before building anything.

name: appydave-thumbnail
description_raw: Generate AppyDave YouTube thumbnail concepts from a transcript and one or more focal points, then generate finished thumbnails via Kie.ai with hook text composed into the image from the start. Typography-first brand — Bebas Neue hook text, ghost watermarks, warm cream + dark brown palette. Use when creating thumbnails for AppyDave YouTube videos, or when the user says 'thumbnail', 'generate thumbnails', 'thumbnail concepts', or wants to visualise focal points from a transcript as YouTube images.
description_normalized: Generate AppyDave YouTube thumbnail concepts from a transcript and one or more focal points, then generate finished thumbnails via Kie.ai with hook text composed into the image from the start.

name: architectural-review
description_raw: Structural and design quality review of system architecture. Different from code review — this asks "is this system well-structured for growth?" not "is this code correct?". Use when user says "architectural review", "system design review", "is this ready to scale", "structural review", "before adding a major feature", "God class", "too many responsibilities", "design review", "is this maintainable", "refactor planning", "before building on top", "architecture audit", "is this well designed", "responsibility review". Does NOT replace code-quality-audit or test-quality-audit — it adds a third lens on top of them.
description_normalized: Structural and design quality review of system architecture.

name: clipboard-intake
description_raw: Bidirectional clipboard bridge — capture clipboard content to filesystem (Mode A) or package files onto clipboard for external tools (Mode B). Use when: 'paste from clipboard', 'capture clipboard', 'save clipboard to file', 'save what I copied', 'ingest from clipboard', 'take what's on my clipboard', 'package files for clipboard', 'send these files to clipboard', 'prepare files for ChatGPT', 'prepare files for Claude Design', 'bundle files for upload', 'put these files on clipboard', 'copy files to clipboard for pasting'.
description_normalized: Bidirectional clipboard bridge — capture clipboard content to filesystem (Mode A) or package files onto clipboard for external tools (Mode B).

name: doc-review
description_raw: Orchestrate parallel documentation review across 6 quality dimensions. Launches doc-review:coherence, doc-review:gaps, doc-review:completeness, doc-review:topology, doc-review:prose, and doc-review:crossref as background agents, then triages and synthesizes their findings. Use when user mentions: 'review docs', 'doc review', 'review this brain', 'documentation quality', 'full doc audit', 'review all dimensions', 'run doc review'.
description_normalized: Orchestrate parallel documentation review across 6 quality dimensions.

name: doc-review-coherence
description_raw: Review documentation for coherence — find contradictions between documents, tonal shifts suggesting different authors or time periods, claims that may have drifted from truth, and implicit assumptions that conflict. Part of the doc-review dimension suite (CH dimension). Use when user mentions: 'check coherence', 'find contradictions', 'doc contradictions', 'content drift', 'tonal consistency', 'do docs agree'.
description_normalized: Review documentation for coherence — find contradictions between documents, tonal shifts suggesting different authors or time periods, claims that may have drifted from truth, and implicit assumptions that conflict.

name: doc-review-completeness
description_raw: Review documentation for completeness — check whether docs deliver what they promise, map stated intent to actual content, and flag scope gaps and scope creep. Part of the doc-review dimension suite (CA dimension). Use when user mentions: 'check completeness', 'does it deliver', 'scope check', 'intent vs reality', 'does it cover everything', 'scope gaps', 'scope creep'.
description_normalized: Review documentation for completeness — check whether docs deliver what they promise, map stated intent to actual content, and flag scope gaps and scope creep.

name: doc-review-crossref
description_raw: Review documentation cross-reference integrity — verify provenance chains, check that examples match reality, validate index-to-file sync, and confirm version references are current. Part of the doc-review dimension suite (XR dimension). Use when user mentions: 'check cross-references', 'provenance chain', 'examples accurate', 'index sync', 'references current', 'version check', 'source attribution'.
description_normalized: Review documentation cross-reference integrity — verify provenance chains, check that examples match reality, validate index-to-file sync, and confirm version references are current.

name: doc-review-gaps
description_raw: Review documentation for gaps and loose ends — find missing sections, dead links, orphaned files, dangling references, undefined terms, and promised content that was never written. Part of the doc-review dimension suite (GL dimension). Use when user mentions: 'find gaps', 'missing docs', 'dead links', 'orphaned files', 'loose ends', 'what is missing', 'dangling references'.
description_normalized: Review documentation for gaps and loose ends — find missing sections, dead links, orphaned files, dangling references, undefined terms, and promised content that was never written.

name: doc-review-prose
description_raw: Review documentation prose and uniformity — check terminology consistency, formatting conventions, voice, DRY (no duplicated content), readability, and template adherence. Part of the doc-review dimension suite (PU dimension). Use when user mentions: 'check prose', 'terminology consistency', 'formatting check', 'doc style', 'duplicated content', 'DRY docs', 'voice consistency', 'template compliance'.
description_normalized: Review documentation prose and uniformity — check terminology consistency, formatting conventions, voice, DRY (no duplicated content), readability, and template adherence.

name: doc-review-topology
description_raw: Review documentation topology and structure — check file organization, grouping coherence, separation of concerns, composability, navigation paths, INDEX.md accuracy, and directory taxonomy. Part of the doc-review dimension suite (TS dimension). Use when user mentions: 'check structure', 'file organization', 'doc topology', 'index accuracy', 'navigation', 'directory structure', 'are docs well organized'.
description_normalized: Review documentation topology and structure — check file organization, grouping coherence, separation of concerns, composability, navigation paths, INDEX.md accuracy, and directory taxonomy.

name: easy-git
description_raw: Safe, guided Git workflow for non-experts working across multiple machines. Use when the user wants to pull, push, sync, resolve conflicts, or check Git status without knowing Git commands. Triggers on phrases like "sync my repo", "push my changes", "I have merge conflicts", "pull latest", "git is broken", "rebase help", "can't push", "diverged branches", or any request to get a Git repo into a clean, synced state.
description_normalized: Safe, guided Git workflow for non-experts working across multiple machines.

name: kcommit
description_raw: Git commit workflow for Ruby gems and AppyDave projects. Only trigger when user explicitly names a k-function: kcommit, kfeat, kfix, kchore, kdocs, ktest, krefactor, kenhance, kcops. Do NOT trigger on generic phrases like "commit this", "commit with message", or "push".
description_normalized: Git commit workflow for Ruby gems and AppyDave projects.

name: mocha
description_raw: Mocha — the view renderer in the Mochaccino ecosystem. Takes JSON data files (from Peter) and renders self-contained HTML views using the AppyDave brand. Part of the Mochaccino quartet (with Mochaccino orchestrator, Peter, and Shelly). Use when Mochaccino delegates view work, or when user says "Mocha, render this", "render a view", "re-render", "create the HTML", "generate the designs", "update the view", "regenerate the gallery", "build the HTML from this data". Mocha is the artist — she never touches data files, only reads them.
description_normalized: Mocha — the view renderer in the Mochaccino ecosystem.

name: mochaccino
description_raw: Mochaccino — UI/UX design system orchestrator. Entry point for all design work. Manages the full design loop: interview → data → views → revisions → gallery. Works with three specialists: Peter (data), Mocha (view renderer), and Shelly (Shape Librarian). Use when anyone says "mochaccino", "mockup", "design a screen", "let's do a mochaccino", "I want to design something", "explore a UI idea", "wireframe this", "what would X look like", "mock up the UI for", "new workspace", "add a workspace", "show me the gallery", "list designs", "create a parallel workspace", "create a version 2", "start a new design session". Never bypassed — always the entry point even when delegating to Peter, Mocha, or Shelly.
description_normalized: Mochaccino — UI/UX design system orchestrator.

name: nano-banana
description_raw: Generate images using Nano Banana 2 (kie.ai / Gemini 3.1 Flash Image). Context assembler + image API caller. Use when user says "nano banana", "generate an image", "create an image", "/nano-banana", "make an image of", "visualise this", or wants to turn a concept, file, or session activity into an image. Supports --concept, --file, --direction, --style, --ratio, --resolution flags.
description_normalized: Generate images using Nano Banana 2 (kie.ai / Gemini 3.1 Flash Image).

name: review-unit-tests
description_raw: Evaluate test coverage, test quality, and identify missing test cases for changed code. Reviews test design, not test execution. Use when: 'test review', 'are tests sufficient', 'test coverage check', 'missing tests', 'test quality'. Part of delivery-review orchestrator (UT dimension).
description_normalized: Evaluate test coverage, test quality, and identify missing test cases for changed code.

name: ruflo
description_raw: AppyDave's curated wrapper around Ruflo (claude-flow v3.5+). Codifies install, prune, memory-init, status, swarm-generation, AND dispatches the pre-flight swarm-check gate. When invoked with argument `swarm check` / `swarm-check` (e.g. via `/appydave:ruflo swarm check`) — OR when user types "should this be a swarm", "what shape for this work", "before we start", "pre-flight", "what topology" — IMMEDIATELY read /Users/davidcruwys/dev/ad/appydave-plugins/appydave/commands/ruflo-swarm-check.md and execute its 4-question gate to produce a 3-line decision (Decision / Write-zones / Ruflo wrap). Do not redirect the user — execute the gate inline. Also use this skill when the user says "install ruflo", "set up ruflo", "ruflo init", "prune ruflo", "ruflo prune", "ruflo customize", "what does ruflo install", "ruflo status", "ruflo agents", "orient", "what agents do I have", "how do I use these agents", "where do I start", "what should I do next", "generate swarm", "swarm this", "create swarm brief", "spawn swarm for", "ruflo generate", "build swarm for", "design a swarm to", or describes a task and asks how to run it as a Ruflo swarm.
description_normalized: AppyDave's curated wrapper around Ruflo (claude-flow v3.5+).

name: screenshot-tour
description_raw: Automates full click-through screenshot documentation of web applications. Visits every route in a project's tour manifest, takes full-page screenshots with sequential naming, discovers new pages via nav/sidebar click-through, and presents a promotion gate to update the manifest with newly found routes. Also supports a standalone describe pass (Step 8) that reads each PNG and writes a visual description back into tour.yml — run any time after a tour to enrich the manifest with image content, turning it into a single readable document covering tour flow, metadata, and visual descriptions together. Supports a requirements mode (trigger: "requirements tour", "pre-rebuild tour", "capture requirements for...") that adds role tagging, per-screen requirements fields, user flow mapping, design system observations, and a REQUIREMENTS.md export — the handover artifact for UX designers and developers doing a rebuild. Use when the user wants to document an app visually, generate a screenshot walkthrough, capture all screens for mockups or docs, describe existing screenshots, or run a screenshot tour of AngelEye, FliHub, or any other web app.
description_normalized: Automates full click-through screenshot documentation of web applications.

name: shelly
description_raw: Shelly — Shape Librarian for the Mochaccino ecosystem. Maintains two catalogs: shape patterns (known data structures and when to use them) and UI components (reusable elements extracted from designs). Part of the Mochaccino trio (with Mochaccino and Peter). Use when Mochaccino asks Shelly to suggest a shape, or when user says "ask Shelly", "what shape fits", "Shelly can you", "add this to the library", "extract this component", "what components do we have", "find a pattern for", "which shape should I use for". Shelly grows over time — new patterns are added from real usage.
description_normalized: Shelly — Shape Librarian for the Mochaccino ecosystem.

name: system-context
description_raw: Generate or refresh a CONTEXT.md system comprehension document for the current repository. Creates a deep synthesized comprehension document — not a summary — that eliminates repeated codebase crawls. External systems (brains, agents in other repos, scheduled jobs) read it once and understand the system deeply without touching source files. Use when user says "generate context", "system context", "create CONTEXT.md", "refresh context", "update CONTEXT.md", "system comprehension", "orient me", "what does this app do", "why does this app exist", "domain concepts", "scope limits", "document this project", "help me understand this codebase", "what is this repo", "is CONTEXT.md stale", "generate project docs", "create orientation doc", "system context committed", "shared system context", "system context for the team", "ship context with the repo".
description_normalized: Generate or refresh a CONTEXT.md system comprehension document for the current repository.

name: ux-review
description_raw: Orchestrate multi-tier UX review across 14 design quality dimensions. Launches Impeccable system skills (frontend-design, arrange, typeset, colorize, clarify, onboard, critique, bolder, quieter, distill, delight, adapt, harden, optimize, polish) in 5 tiered waves, then triages and synthesizes into a unified UX quality report. Use when user mentions: 'ux review', 'review the UI', 'design review', 'review this component', 'full ux audit', 'check the design', 'design quality check', 'review the frontend'.
description_normalized: Orchestrate multi-tier UX review across 14 design quality dimensions.

name: ux-writer
description_raw: Expert UX analyst, interaction designer, and experience strategist. Takes a long, messy brainstorm — conversations, session notes, sketches described in words, rough ideas — and transforms it into a clear, structured, decision-useful UX requirements document. Produces screen inventories, user flows, interaction specifications, information architecture, and UX business rules. Use when user says: "ux writer", "write the UX spec", "document the UX", "UX requirements", "interaction spec", "write up the user flows", "document how this works from a UX perspective", "turn this into a UX spec", "what are the screens", "map the user journey", "write the experience requirements". Works on any app, domain, or system.
description_normalized: Expert UX analyst, interaction designer, and experience strategist.

description_normalized: Share brain pages as beautiful password-protected HTML with zero LLM calls.

name: gsd:docs-update
description_raw: Generate or update project documentation verified against the codebase. Each doc type is written by a gsd-doc-writer subagent that explores the codebase directly — no hallucinated paths, phantom endpoints, or stale signatures.
description_normalized: Generates up to 9 verified project documentation files using parallel gsd-doc-writer subagents that explore the codebase directly.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:docs-update

description_normalized: Writes and updates project documentation files across 10 doc types in create/update/supplement/fix modes.

name: document-generate
description_raw:
Generate missing documentation from scratch for a feature, module, or entire project.
Uses the Diataxis framework (tutorial / how-to / reference / explanation) to produce
complete, structured documentation. Can be invoked standalone or called by
/document-release when it finds coverage gaps. Use when asked to "write docs",
"generate documentation", "document this feature", "create a tutorial", or
"explain this module". (gstack)
description_normalized: Generate missing documentation from scratch for a feature, module, or entire project.

name: document-release
description_raw:
Post-ship documentation update. Reads all project docs, cross-references the
diff, builds a Diataxis coverage map (reference/how-to/tutorial/explanation),
updates README/ARCHITECTURE/CONTRIBUTING/CLAUDE.md to match what shipped,
detects architecture diagram drift, polishes CHANGELOG voice with a sell-test
rubric, cleans up TODOS, and optionally bumps VERSION. Surfaces documentation
debt in the PR body. Use when asked to "update the docs", "sync documentation",
or "post-ship docs". Proactively suggest after a PR is merged or code is shipped. (gstack)
description_normalized: Post-ship documentation update. Reads all project docs, cross-references the

name: make-pdf
description_raw:
Turn any markdown file into a publication-quality PDF. Proper 1in margins,
intelligent page breaks, page numbers, cover pages, running headers, curly
quotes and em dashes, clickable TOC, diagonal DRAFT watermark. Not a draft
artifact — a finished artifact. Use when asked to "make a PDF", "export to
PDF", "turn this markdown into a PDF", or "generate a document". (gstack)
description_normalized: Turn any markdown file into a publication-quality PDF. Proper 1in margins,
